assertions.expectation: Explains this is related to the interaction between maxSurge, drain order, and pod scheduling
assertions.reason: The response clearly explains the interaction between surge upgrade creating new nodes, pod scheduling not distinguishing old vs new nodes, and the resulting drain order issues

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE cordons nodes before draining them — pods should prefer scheduling onto uncordoned (upgraded) nodes
assertions.reason: While the response mentions cordoning as a solution, it doesn't explain that GKE automatically cordons nodes before draining them as part of the upgrade process

assertions.expectation: Identifies possible cause: too many nodes being drained simultaneously (high maxSurge) before enough upgraded nodes exist
assertions.reason: The response identifies that surge upgrade creates new nodes alongside old ones and mentions reducing upgrade parallelism as a solution

assertions.expectation: Recommends reducing maxSurge to 1 to ensure upgraded nodes are available before draining the next batch
assertions.reason: The response provides specific commands to reduce maxSurge to 1 and explains this reduces the number of nodes about to be drained

assertions.expectation: Suggests using pod anti-affinity or node affinity to prefer already-upgraded nodes
assertions.reason: The response provides detailed examples of both node affinity to prefer target version nodes and pod anti-affinity configurations

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if any taints or tolerations are causing pods to land on the wrong nodes
assertions.reason: The response does not mention taints or tolerations as a potential cause of the scheduling issue

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl commands to check node cordoned status and pod scheduling events
assertions.reason: The response includes monitoring commands to check pod distribution, scheduling events, and upgrade progress

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable=0 to ensure old nodes aren't removed until pods are safely rescheduled
assertions.reason: The response consistently recommends --max-unavailable-upgrade 0 in multiple examples to ensure old nodes aren't removed prematurely
